description:
PRD: Workspace Management and Switching Flow

## Problem Statement

Beadsmith currently determines the Current Workspace from the process working directory, set once at launch and never changed at runtime. There is no in-app mechanism to select, switch, or persist workspaces. The sidebar shows the workspace path as static, read-only text with a decorative switcher icon. The user has no way to manage multiple Beadwork projects from within the app.

## Solution

Replace the process-cwd workspace model with explicit workspace management. The backend holds an active Workspace in state, persists known Workspaces via tauri-plugin-store, and threads the workspace path explicitly through each bw subprocess call. The sidebar footer becomes an interactive workspace picker showing recent Workspaces and an Add New action. A prominent empty state guides first-time users to add their first Workspace.

## User Stories

1. As a developer, I want to see my current Workspace name and path in the sidebar footer, so that I always know which project I am looking at
2. As a developer, I want to click the sidebar footer to open a dropdown of my recent Workspaces, so that I can quickly switch between projects
3. As a developer, I want each Workspace in the dropdown to show its name and full path, so that I can distinguish between projects with the same directory name
4. As a developer, I want my most recently used Workspaces to appear at the top of the dropdown, so that I can reach my active projects fastest
5. As a developer, I want the currently active Workspace to be highlighted in the dropdown, so that I can see which one I am on
6. As a developer, I want to click a Workspace in the dropdown to switch to it, so that the Issue Explorer loads that project's issues
7. As a developer, I want the dropdown to close immediately after I select a Workspace, so that the UI feels responsive
8. As a developer, I want the sidebar footer to update to the new Workspace name immediately after switching, so that I get instant confirmation of the switch
9. As a developer, I want the Issue Explorer to show a loading state while switching Workspaces, so that I do not see stale issues from the previous project
10. As a developer, I want to add a new Workspace by clicking an Add New button in the dropdown, so that I can register a project I have not used before
11. As a developer, I want Add New to open a native OS folder picker, so that I can browse my filesystem and select the right directory
12. As a developer, I want the dropdown to close before the folder picker opens, so that the UI is not cluttered
13. As a developer, I want Beadsmith to validate that the selected directory is a Beadwork workspace before adding it, so that I do not add broken entries
14. As a developer, I want to see an error message if I select a directory that is not a Beadwork workspace, so that I understand what went wrong
15. As a developer, I want adding a Workspace to automatically switch to it, so that I can immediately start working in the new project
16. As a developer, I want Beadsmith to remember my Workspaces across restarts, so that I do not have to re-add them every time I launch the app
17. As a developer, I want Beadsmith to remember which Workspace was last active, so that it opens to the same project I was working in
18. As a developer, on first launch with no Workspace configured, I want to see a prominent empty state with an Add Workspace button, so that I know what to do
19. As a developer, I want the empty state to explain what a Workspace is, so that I understand the concept before adding one
20. As a developer, I want the empty state to hint that I can also switch Workspaces from the sidebar, so that I learn about the second entry point
21. As a developer, I want the empty state Add Workspace button to open the native folder picker directly, so that I can get started in one click
22. As a developer, I want to see an inline error on a Workspace row if its directory has been moved or deleted, so that I understand why switching failed
23. As a developer, I want a failed Workspace row to remain selectable, so that I can retry switching in case the directory was temporarily unavailable
24. As a developer, I want the active Workspace to remain unchanged when a switch fails, so that I am not left looking at a broken project
25. As a developer, I want re-adding a Workspace I already have to just switch to it, so that duplicate paths do not create confusion
26. As a developer, I want Beadsmith to silently handle the 100-Workspace cap by evicting the oldest entry, so that I never hit a hard limit error
27. As a developer, I want the Workspace name to default to the directory basename, so that the picker shows something readable without manual naming
28. As a developer, I want the stored Workspace name to persist on disk rather than being derived at runtime, so that the displayed name is stable

## Implementation Decisions

### Architectural Decisions (ADR-0006)

- Thread the workspace path explicitly through each bw subprocess call via Command::new("bw").current_dir(workspace_path) instead of relying on process-global cwd
- The backend holds the active Workspace in memory and persists it (along with the recent-Workspaces list) using tauri-plugin-store
- Remove process cwd entirely from the workspace resolution path — no env::current_dir, no --workspace CLI flag, no workspace.rs override module
- On launch, the backend reads the last active Workspace from the store; if no valid Workspace is stored, the frontend shows an empty state

### RPC Contract Changes

New commands added to the BeadsmithApi trait:
- list_workspaces() -> Vec<WorkspaceEntry> — returns stored entries ordered by lastOpenedAt descending
- add_workspace(path: String) -> Result<WorkspaceEntry, WorkspaceError> — validates via bw config list, creates a WorkspaceEntry { path, name: basename(path), lastOpenedAt: now }, stores it, returns it. Re-adding an existing path is a no-op that refreshes lastOpenedAt and returns the existing entry. Does NOT switch.
- validate_workspace(path: String) -> Result<(), WorkspaceError> — runs bw config list in the given directory, returns Ok or an error
- switch_workspace(path: String) -> Result<LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse, IssueListError> — validates the path internally (bw config list), updates lastOpenedAt, sets as active, loads issue data. If validation fails, returns IssueListError and the active Workspace is unchanged. Returns the same response shape as load_issue_explorer_data.

Existing commands (unchanged signatures):
- load_issue_explorer_data() -> Result<LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse, IssueListError> — backend reads its active Workspace internally
- list_issues() -> Result<ListIssuesResponse, IssueListError> — backend reads its active Workspace internally

New shared type:
- WorkspaceEntry { path: String, name: String, lastOpenedAt: String } — serialized as camelCase across the TauRPC boundary

### Error Types

IssueListError gains a new variant: NoActiveWorkspace — returned by load_issue_explorer_data when no active Workspace is set. The frontend routes this to the empty state.

New WorkspaceError enum (returned by validate_workspace and add_workspace) with variants:
- NotBeadworkWorkspace — bw config list ran but the directory is not a Beadwork workspace
- MissingBinary — bw not found on PATH
- CommandFailed — bw config list exited non-zero for an unexpected reason
- Io — filesystem error (directory does not exist, permission denied)
- StoreError — tauri-plugin-store read/write failed

### Validation

Workspace validation uses bw config list (the lightest bw command that confirms Beadwork initialization, ~8ms). The adapter reuses the existing NOT_BEADWORK_MARKERS detection (not a git repository, beadwork not initialized). switch_workspace validates internally before switching — one atomic call, no race condition between separate validate and switch calls.

### Workspace Entry Shape

Each entry stores { path, name, lastOpenedAt }. Name defaults to the directory basename at creation time and is stored on disk (not derived at runtime). lastOpenedAt is an RFC3339 timestamp updated on every switch. Entries are ordered by lastOpenedAt descending. The list caps at 100 entries; when full, adding a new entry silently evicts the one with the oldest lastOpenedAt.

### Backend State

The backend maintains an active workspace path in memory, initialized on launch from the store's lastActivePath. This is app-level state (not Workspace-level), persisted via tauri-plugin-store. The --workspace CLI flag and workspace.rs override module are removed entirely.

### Frontend State

IssueExplorerLoadState gains a noWorkspace variant (or the existing failure state carries a NoActiveWorkspace error kind). The frontend calls load_issue_explorer_data on mount as today; NoActiveWorkspace routes to the empty state instead of the error display. The workspace dropdown state (open/closed, inline errors on rows) is local React component state.

### UI: Sidebar Workspace Picker

The sidebar footer Current Workspace block becomes an interactive dropdown trigger. Clicking it opens a popover anchored to the footer. The dropdown lists recent Workspaces ordered by lastOpenedAt descending. Each row shows the Workspace name (bold, primary text) and full path (muted, truncated, secondary text). The active Workspace is highlighted. An Add New button sits at the bottom of the dropdown. Selecting a Workspace closes the dropdown and triggers switch_workspace. The sidebar footer updates to the new Workspace name immediately.

### UI: Add New Flow

Add New closes the dropdown, then opens a native OS folder picker via tauri-plugin-dialog. If the user cancels the picker, nothing happens — they return to their current state. If the user picks a directory, Beadsmith validates it (bw config list), adds it to the store (add_workspace), then switches to it (switch_workspace). Both the empty-state button and the dropdown Add New trigger this same flow.

### UI: Empty State

When no Workspace is configured (first launch or invalid stored path), the middle pane shows a prominent empty state: a large workspace/folder icon, a heading (No workspace configured), a one-line description, a primary Add Workspace button that opens the native folder picker directly, and a subtle hint about the sidebar entry point.

### UI: Switch Failure

When switch_workspace fails validation, an inline error appears on the failed Workspace row in the dropdown (the name/path dims or turns red with a small error message below). The dropdown stays open. The failed row remains selectable for retry. The active Workspace is unchanged.

### E2e Test Infrastructure

The --workspace CLI flag is removed. E2e tests must populate the store with a Workspace path before the app loads issues, using the same API a real user would (or a test-only store seed). The existing e2e/issue-list/ suite fixtures and scenario runner are updated accordingly. New e2e specs cover: empty state on first launch, adding a Workspace, switching between Workspaces, and inline error on stale Workspace.

## Testing Decisions

### What makes a good test

Tests should assert on external behavior, not implementation details. A good test proves that the system does the right thing from the user's perspective (correct data flows, correct UI states, correct error handling) without coupling to internal wiring.

### Test seams

1. CommandRunner trait (existing, modified) — the central backend seam. The trait gains a workspace path parameter (or the adapter threads it before calling run). Unit tests in the adapter and RPC layers inject a FakeRunner with canned outputs and assert the correct workspace path is threaded to bw calls. This is the highest backend seam: it proves the backend runs bw in the right directory without needing a real filesystem or bw binary. Prior art: adapter.rs and rpc.rs FakeRunner tests.

2. WorkspaceStore seam (new) — a thin abstraction over tauri-plugin-store (a trait like WorkspaceStore) so workspace management commands can be unit-tested with a fake store. Mirrors the CommandRunner pattern. Tests assert on stored entries, ordering, cap eviction, and re-add no-op behavior.

3. Frontend workspace state (existing patterns, extended) — IssueExplorerLoadState gains a noWorkspace variant. The existing issue-loader.test.ts pattern (inject a fake IssueTransport, assert on state) and App.test.tsx pattern (mock loadIssueExplorerStateFromTauRpc, assert on rendered output) extend to cover the empty state, workspace dropdown, and switch flow.

4. E2e WebDriver (existing, modified) — the highest integration seam. The existing e2e/issue-list/ suite launches the real built binary. New specs cover: empty state on first launch, adding a Workspace via the picker, switching between Workspaces, and inline error on a stale Workspace. Existing specs are updated to populate the store instead of passing --workspace. Prior art: e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts and e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts.

## Out of Scope

- Renaming Workspaces (the name field exists in WorkspaceEntry and defaults to basename, but no UI or command to change it)
- Removing Workspaces from the recent list
- Autocomplete filesystem path typing in the Add New flow (native folder picker only for this slice; typed autocomplete can come later)
- Terminal-launch auto-detection (launching from a directory no longer auto-detects the Workspace; could return as a future convenience)
- Command Palette workspace switching (Cmd+K flow from the PRD is a separate feature)
- Workspace-scoped settings or preferences
- Multiple Workspace tabs or split views

## Further Notes

- ADR-0006 (docs/adr/0006-workspace-management-and-switching.md) documents the architectural decisions for this feature
- CONTEXT.md has been updated with Workspace and Current Workspace domain terms
- The tauri-plugin-store dependency is added via pnpm tauri add store
- The tauri-plugin-dialog dependency is added via pnpm tauri add dialog
- bw config list was chosen as the validation command for being the lightest bw call (~8ms) that confirms Beadwork initialization, reusing the existing NOT_BEADWORK_MARKERS detection
title: Workspace Management and Switching Flow
